---
title: "Title of Section 1"
---

<!-- You have to develop your own headline structure. -->

## Data Preprocessing
### Data importing
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import zipfile # For handling zip files

# Upload zip.folder from ESS website 
zip_path = "ESS9e03_3-ESS10e03_3-ESS10SCe03_2-ESS11e04_1-subset.zip"
csv_inside = "ESS9e03_3-ESS10e03_3-ESS10SCe03_2-ESS11e04_1-subset.csv"

with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_path, 'r') as z:
        with z.open(csv_inside) as f:
            df = pd.read_csv(f, low_memory=False)

print("✅ CSV Load Successful!")
print(f"Dataset Size: {df.shape[0]} rows x {df.shape[1]} columns")
```

### Data Exploration
```{python}

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

# Dataset Dimensions (Rows, Columns)
print(f"### 1. Dataset Dimensions")
print(f"The dataset contains {df.shape[0]} respondents and {df.shape[1]} variables.")
# Data Structure
print("\n### 2. Data Structure")
print("Column Names (First 50):")
print(df.columns.tolist()[:50])

# 3. Raw Data Preview
print("\n### 3. Raw Data Preview (Selected Columns)")
# pick only the columns of interest to display: country, birth year, and some human values questions
interest_cols = ['cntry', 'yrbrn'] + [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith('ip')][:5]
print(df[interest_cols].head())

# Statistical Summary
print("\n### 4. Statistical Summary of Values & Birth Year")
key_vars = ['yrbrn'] + [c for c in df.columns if c.startswith('ip')][:10]
display(df[key_vars].describe().T)

# Diversity
print("\n### 5. Country Distribution (Top 10)")
display(df['cntry'].value_counts().head(10))

```



### Data Cleansing
```{python}
#Missing Values


```

## Headline 2
